notes: One kiro-cli session and one claudecode session driven concurrently in the SAME cwd by run-cell-multi.sh, observed by a single --record daemon. The daemon labels each adapter independently (kiro-cli Process.Match=ExactName{kiro-cli} + Source=FilesUnderRoot{.kiro/sessions/cli}, disjoint from claudecode's matcher and ~/.claude/projects root), so the workspace events.jsonl carries two distinct sessions — one per adapter — each with its own working->ready arc and no cross-contamination. This spec is adapter-agnostic: agentA is whichever session enters working first, agentB the other; both cells (kiro-cli + claudecode) share this workspace events.jsonl (each cell differs only in which transcript it stores for the replay golden). Every phase anchors on a WORKING transition, never a ready, because the adapters emit a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready that never works — anchoring on working skips those placeholders, and new_session on agentB skips agentA's already-matched session. Asserts: two distinct sessions, each an independent working->ready arc, in one workspace. kiro-cli's JSONL has no model/token/cost, so observations omit model (live recording 2026-06-05-05-19-02_irrlichd-0.4.8+d458c2e.dirty proved the model field is empty/absent, not the doc-placeholder "unknown"), cost/tokens zero.

text: First agent's turn drives ready -> working (binds agentA; skips both adapters' born-ready proc-<PID> placeholders by anchoring on working)
First agent's turn completes: working -> ready
The OTHER agent (a DISTINCT session, different adapter, same cwd) drives ready -> working independently (new_session proves it is not agentA; working anchor skips the placeholders)
Second agent's turn completes: working -> ready — two agents, one workspace, labeled independently with no cross-contamination
